title: Server Layer Tests (Critical Path)
impact: Tests entry point for all 47 MCP tools

tasks.description: Create toolHandlers unit tests - Test all 47 tool handler functions including entity operations, relation operations, observation operations, search operations, tag operations, hierarchy operations, and error handling
testCategories: Error Handling (unknown tool, invalid input, error details)
acceptanceCriteria: Error cases tested for each category

tasks.description: Create toolDefinitions validation tests - Test schema validation for all tools, verify tool categories and naming conventions, validate input constraints
testCategories: Schema Validation (valid JSON schema, mandatory fields, naming convention)
Tool Categories (47 tool definitions, category organization, unique names)
Input Schemas (entity name constraints, importance range 0-10, tag array format)
acceptanceCriteria: Schema validation tests for all tools

tasks.description: Create MCPServer integration tests - Test server initialization, tool registration, MCP protocol handling, response formatting, and concurrent requests
Create responseFormatter tests - Test formatToolResponse, formatTextResponse, and formatRawResponse functions with success/error cases and edge conditions
testCategories: formatRawResponse (pass through raw data, complex objects)

successCriteria: Server module coverage increases from 16% to 85%
notes: This is the critical path sprint. The server layer is the entry point for all MCP tools and has the lowest coverage (16%). Testing this layer ensures all 47 tools work correctly before testing the underlying managers.
